skill.name: senior-frontend
skill.description: Frontend development guidance for React, Next.js, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, component scaffolding, bundle analysis, and code review workflows.
skill.summary: Build and review modern React and Next.js frontend work with practical performance and architecture guidance.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: AI review found the high-risk static findings to be false positives from markdown examples and generic text. The only confirmed issue is local filesystem output in the helper scripts, which is user-directed and does not show malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Optional Local File Write in Helper Scripts
medium_findings.description: The three Python helper scripts accept an --output path and write JSON results to that user-supplied path. This is a real filesystem capability, but it does not include network access, secret access, or command execution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The scripts directly open args.output for writing. The risk is limited because the path is explicitly provided by the user and no remote sink is present.

low_findings.title: Documented Shell Commands Are Not Executed by the Skill
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged bash snippets in SKILL.md and TypeScript fences in reference files as command execution. These are documentation examples and there is no evidence that the skill executes them automatically.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text appears inside markdown code fences or prose. No subprocess, eval, or automatic execution path is present in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto, Environment Access, and Reconnaissance Flags Lack Evidence
low_findings.description: The reviewed locations do not contain weak cryptographic algorithms, secret reads, or host reconnaissance behavior. The .env line is a setup example copying a sample file, and the reconnaissance flag points to reference prose.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain descriptions, setup commands, or headings rather than cryptographic operations or system enumeration. No evidence found for the scanner labels.

dangerous_patterns.title: Optional Local File Write in Helper Scripts
dangerous_patterns.description: The three Python helper scripts accept an --output path and write JSON results to that user-supplied path. This is a real filesystem capability, but it does not include network access, secret access, or command execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The scripts directly open args.output for writing. The risk is limited because the path is explicitly provided by the user and no remote sink is present.

content.value_statement: Modern frontend work often needs consistent React patterns, fast reviews, and practical optimization guidance. This skill guides component design, scaffolding, bundle review, and frontend best practices.
actual_capabilities: Guides React, Next.js, TypeScript, and Tailwind CSS implementation choices.
Documents commands for component generation, bundle analysis, and frontend scaffolding scripts.
Provides reference material for frontend patterns, optimization, and best practices.
Supports frontend code review with performance, security, and maintainability criteria.
Can run local Python helper scripts that validate a target path and print a simple report.
limitations: The helper scripts are minimal templates and do not perform deep bundle inspection.
The reference files contain generic examples rather than project-specific architecture rules.
The skill does not include automated dependency upgrades or framework migrations.
The scripts can write reports only to paths provided by the user.

use_cases.title: Review React Feature Quality
use_cases.description: Use the skill to evaluate component structure, state handling, accessibility, and maintainability before merge.

use_cases.title: Plan a Next.js Optimization Pass
use_cases.description: Use the guidance to identify likely performance bottlenecks and organize a focused improvement plan.

use_cases.title: Scaffold Consistent UI Work
use_cases.description: Use the documented scripts and practices to start frontend tasks with consistent project conventions.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Component
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of one frontend component.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this React component for readability, accessibility, state management, and TypeScript usage. List the highest impact fixes first.

prompt_templates.title: Improve a Feature Flow
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review of a user-facing workflow.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this frontend feature flow. Suggest changes for component boundaries, loading states, error handling, and responsive behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize a Next.js Page
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced performance review for a page or route.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Next.js page for rendering strategy, bundle size, data fetching, caching, and user experience performance.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Frontend Refactor Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced planning across multiple frontend modules.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a staged refactor plan for this frontend area. Include risks, tests, migration order, and rollback considerations.

output_examples.input: Review a TypeScript React component before a pull request.
output_examples.output: A prioritized review covering props, rendering behavior, accessibility gaps, state placement, test coverage, and suggested follow-up tasks.

output_examples.input: Plan performance improvements for a Next.js route.
output_examples.output: A concise optimization plan covering rendering mode, bundle contributors, image handling, caching, and measurement steps.

output_examples.input: Create frontend implementation guidance for a new dashboard view.
output_examples.output: A practical build outline with component boundaries, data states, layout behavior, styling conventions, and validation checks.

best_practices: Run helper scripts only on trusted project paths and review output paths before writing reports.
Pair the guidance with project-specific linting, tests, accessibility checks, and performance measurements.
Treat generated recommendations as review input, then verify behavior in the actual application.
anti_patterns: Do not assume the scripts perform complete bundle analysis without adding project-specific logic.
Do not apply generic React patterns without checking existing architecture and team conventions.
Do not write script output to sensitive or important files without reviewing the target path.

faq.question: Does this skill execute deployment commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. Deployment commands appear as documentation examples. The reviewed scripts do not run external commands.

faq.question: Can it analyze any React or Next.js project?
faq.answer: It can guide reviews for many projects, but the included scripts are simple templates.

faq.question: Does it access environment secrets?
faq.answer: No evidence found. The .env reference is a setup example, not secret reading logic.

faq.question: Why is the risk level low?
faq.answer: The scripts can write a local JSON report to a user-selected path. No malicious behavior was confirmed.

faq.question: Is this useful for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill content is compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.

faq.question: What should users verify before publishing generated changes?
faq.answer: Users should run tests, lint checks, accessibility checks, and real performance measurements in their project.
